metadata.invention_title: System and Method for Optimization through Strategic Elimination with Paradox Retention

problem: Traditional optimization (e.g., Monte Carlo algorithms) are inefficient in complex, high-dimensional domains.
Common issues include poor convergence, local minima traps, and wasted computation on non-viable solution paths.
need: Modern problems like protein folding and large-scale AI demand better efficiency and higher-order logic.
No prior method systematically preserves paradox (contradictory yet promising candidates) during optimization.

summary_of_invention.description: The Forgetting Engine treats elimination—not search—as the core computational act. At each iteration, non-viable candidates are aggressively pruned, while contradictory (paradoxical) candidates with potential are preserved for enhanced discovery.
innovations: Strategic Elimination as a computational primitive
Paradox Retention Mechanism enabled by metrics (SCS, EPI, DCM)
Dual-gated optimization loop with retention and elimination gates

phases: Initialization: Generate a population of N candidates (optimal N≈50)
Strategic Elimination: Remove 20–40% worst performing candidates
Paradox Retention: Preserve contradictory/paradoxical candidates with long-term promise
Population Management: Replenish population via mutation/recombination

paradox_metrics.name: SCS
paradox_metrics.description: Systemic Coherence Score, measures overall contribution to solution objective

paradox_metrics.name: EPI
paradox_metrics.description: Energetic Paradox Index, quantifies tension between immediate and future utility

paradox_metrics.name: DCM
paradox_metrics.description: Diversity Contribution Metric, balances memory cost vs. opportunity cost

experimental_validation.benchmark: Protein Folding (2D Lattice HP Model, 20 residues)

comparisons.note: Searches, doesn't eliminate non-viables with paradox retention
Maintains diversity, but less aggressive pruning
Single solution focus, no population with paradox retention

applications: Therapeutics, creative AI, business process optimization

philosophy.core_thesis: Elimination—not accumulation or search—is the fundamental computational act in optimization and intelligence.
philosophy.paradox: Forgetting can systematically produce better solutions than remembering in complex, high-entropy domains.

patent_claims: Iterative elimination of worst-performing candidates with paradoxical candidate retention.
Explicit paradox metrics for elimination/retention decisions.
Demonstrable improvement over Monte Carlo and genetic methods.
